title: Normalized incident-window record (TR-02)
description: The vendor-neutral shape EVERY telemetry backend's `window` subcommand emits, one JSON object per NDJSON line. It carries EXACTLY the manifest §12 discovered-side field names so TR-03 joins one schema regardless of source (OTLP-JSON / CloudWatch / Dynatrace). `event_name` is the §12 join key. A record MISSING `event_name` is still schema-valid (it is bucketed DARK-in-prod by TR-03, never dropped). There is deliberately NO manifest_journey_id — telemetry has no design-time IDs; TR-03 DERIVES the journey by matching event_name. `emitter` is consumed by TR-loop-guard to exclude self-emitted events. Enums (vital_class, env, severity, required-string types) are the Norway-guard: a boolean/null in a string slot is schema-invalid, asserted via `uv run` jsonschema (ADR 0014/0015).

event_name.description: The §12 join key — the SAME string the implementer emits at runtime and the manifest declares on the vital step. Absent => DARK-in-prod (unjoinable), bucketed by TR-03, never dropped.

vital_class.description: §4/§12 vital classification, verbatim from the manifest step enum. `null` (explicit) is a non-vital emission; absent is treated as unknown by TR-03.

env.description: Deployment environment — the §4 `environments` primitive reserved set (a boolean/typo here is schema-invalid: the env-reserved Norway-guard).

timestamp.description: RFC3339 emission time. Used for windowing/ordering ONLY; TR-loop-guard's dedupe key deliberately EXCLUDES it so incident retries collapse.

trace_id.description: OTEL trace id (ADR 0006 correlation). Optional; passthrough.

span_id.description: OTEL span id (ADR 0006 correlation). Optional; passthrough.

severity.description: Normalized severity. Contextualizes (never invents) the criticality ladder in TR-03.

count.description: Occurrence count within the bounded window (spike contextualization).

attributes.description: Backend passthrough bag — IGNORED by the join. Present so no field is silently dropped on normalization.

emitter.description: The producing service/agent identity. TR-loop-guard drops records whose emitter matches the triage tier's own identities (config loop_guard.self_emitters) so the tier cannot triage its own emissions.
